# systemd

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `systemd`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `systemd.additionalUpstreamSystemUnits` | `list of string` | Additional units shipped with systemd that shall be enabled. |
| `systemd.automounts` | `list of (submodule)` | Definition of systemd automount units; see {manpage}`systemd.automount(5)`. This is a list instead of an attrSet, because systemd mandates the names to be derived from the `where` attribute. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.after` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay this unit until they have started. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.aliases` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Aliases of that unit. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.automountConfig` | `attribute set of (systemd option)` | Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the `[Automount]` section of the unit. See {manpage}`systemd.automount(5)` for details. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.before` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay them until this unit has started. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.bindsTo` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Like â€˜requiresâ€™, but in addition, if the specified units unexpectedly disappear, this unit will be stopped as well. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.conflicts` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are started, then this unit is stopped and vice versa. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.description` | `(optionally newline-terminated) single-line string` | Description of this unit used in systemd messages and progress indicators. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.documentation` | `list of string` | A list of URIs referencing documentation for this unit or its configuration. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.enable` | `boolean` | If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. `serial-getty@ttyS0`) from being started. Note that `enable=true` does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see `wantedBy`. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.name` | `string` | The name of this systemd unit, including its extension. This can be used to refer to this unit from other systemd units. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.onFailure` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "failed" state. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.onSuccess` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "inactive" state. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.overrideStrategy` | `one of "asDropinIfExists", "asDropin"` | Defines how unit configuration is provided for systemd: `asDropinIfExists` creates a unit file when no unit file is provided by the package otherwise it creates a drop-in file named `overrides.conf`. `asDropin` creates a drop-in file named `overrides.conf`. Mainly needed to define instances for systemd template units (e.g. `systemd-nspawn@mycontainer.service`). See also {manpage}`systemd.unit(5)`. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.partOf` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are stopped or restarted, then this unit is stopped or restarted as well. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.reloadTriggers` | `list of (systemd option)` | An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be reloaded. If anything but a reload trigger changes in the unit file, the unit will be restarted instead. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.requiredBy` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Units that require (i.e. depend on and need to go down with) this unit. As discussed in the `wantedBy` option description this also creates `.requires` symlinks automatically. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.requires` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Start the specified units when this unit is started, and stop this unit when the specified units are stopped or fail. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.requisite` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Similar to requires. However if the units listed are not started, they will not be started and the transaction will fail. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.restartTriggers` | `list of unspecified value` | An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be restarted. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.startLimitBurst` | `signed integer` | Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.startLimitIntervalSec` | `signed integer` | Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.unitConfig` | `attribute set of (systemd option)` | Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the `[Unit]` section of the unit. See {manpage}`systemd.unit(5)` for details. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.upheldBy` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Keep this unit running as long as the listed units are running. This is a continuously enforced version of wantedBy. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.upholds` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Keeps the specified running while this unit is running. A continuous version of `wants`. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.wantedBy` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Units that want (i.e. depend on) this unit. The default method for starting a unit by default at boot time is to set this option to `["multi-user.target"]` for system services. Likewise for user units (`systemd.user.<name>.*`) set it to `["default.target"]` to make a unit start by default when the user `<name>` logs on. This option creates a `.wants` symlink in the given target that exists statelessly without the need for running `systemctl enable`. The `[Install]` section described in {manpage}`systemd.unit(5)` however is not supported because it is a stateful process that does not fit well into the NixOS design. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.wants` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Start the specified units when this unit is started. |
| `systemd.automounts.*.where` | `string` | Absolute path of a directory of the mount point. Will be created if it doesn't exist. (Mandatory) |
| `systemd.coredump.enable` | `boolean` | Whether core dumps should be processed by {command}`systemd-coredump`. If disabled, core dumps appear in the current directory of the crashing process. |
| `systemd.coredump.settings.Coredump` | `open submodule of attribute set of (systemd option)` | Settings for systemd-coredump. See {manpage}`coredump.conf(5)` for available options. |
| `systemd.ctrlAltDelUnit` | `string` | Target that should be started when Ctrl-Alt-Delete is pressed; see {manpage}`systemd.special(7)`. |
| `systemd.defaultUnit` | `string` | Default unit started when the system boots; see {manpage}`systemd.special(7)`. |
| `systemd.enableEmergencyMode` | `boolean` | Whether to enable emergency mode, which is an {command}`sulogin` shell started on the console if mounting a filesystem fails. Since some machines (like EC2 instances) have no console of any kind, emergency mode doesn't make sense, and it's better to continue with the boot insofar as possible. For initrd emergency access, use boot.initrd.systemd.emergencyAccess instead. |
| `systemd.enableStrictShellChecks` | `boolean` | Whether to run `shellcheck` on the generated scripts for systemd units. When enabled, all systemd scripts generated by NixOS will be checked with `shellcheck` and any errors or warnings will cause the build to fail. This affects all scripts that have been created through the `script`, `reload`, `preStart`, `postStart`, `preStop` and `postStop` options for systemd services. This does not affect command lines passed directly to `ExecStart`, `ExecReload`, `ExecStartPre`, `ExecStartPost`, `ExecStop` or `ExecStopPost`. It therefore also does not affect systemd units that are coming from packages and that are not defined through the NixOS config. This option is disabled by default, and although some services have already been fixed, it is still likely that you will encounter build failures when enabling this. We encourage people to enable this option when they are willing and able to submit fixes for potential build failures to Nixpkgs. The option can also be enabled or disabled for individual services using the `enableStrictShellChecks` option on the service itself, which will take precedence over the global setting. |
| `systemd.generatorEnvironment` | `attribute set of string` | Environment variables for systemd generators. The `PATH` environment variable is populated via `systemd.generatorPath`. |
| `systemd.generatorPath` | `list of package` | Packages added to the `PATH` environment variable of all systemd generators. |
| `systemd.generators` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Definition of systemd generators; see {manpage}`systemd.generator(5)`. For each `NAME = VALUE` pair of the attrSet, a link is generated from `/etc/systemd/system-generators/NAME` to `VALUE`. |
| `systemd.globalEnvironment` | `attribute set of (null or string or absolute path or package)` | Environment variables passed to *all* systemd units. |
| `systemd.managerEnvironment` | `attribute set of (null or string or absolute path or package)` | Environment variables of PID 1. These variables are *not* passed to started units. |

| `systemd.network.config` | `submodule` | Definition of global systemd network config. |
| `systemd.network.config.addRouteTablesToIPRoute2` | `boolean` | If true and routeTables are set, then the specified route tables will also be installed into /etc/iproute2/rt_tables. |

| `systemd.network.wait-online.anyInterface` | `boolean` | Whether to consider the network online when any interface is online, as opposed to all of them. This is useful on portable machines with a wired and a wireless interface, for example. This is on by default if {option}`networking.useDHCP` is enabled. |
| `systemd.network.wait-online.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the systemd-networkd-wait-online service. systemd-networkd-wait-online can timeout and fail if there are no network interfaces available for it to manage. When systemd-networkd is enabled but a different service is responsible for managing the system's internet connection (for example, NetworkManager or connman are used to manage WiFi connections), this service is unnecessary and can be disabled. |
| `systemd.network.wait-online.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Extra command-line arguments to pass to systemd-networkd-wait-online. These also affect per-interface `systemd-network-wait-online@` services. See {manpage}`systemd-networkd-wait-online.service(8)` for all available options. |
| `systemd.network.wait-online.ignoredInterfaces` | `list of string` | Network interfaces to be ignored when deciding if the system is online. |
| `systemd.network.wait-online.timeout` | `unsigned integer, meaning >=0` | Time to wait for the network to come online, in seconds. Set to 0 to disable. |
| `systemd.nspawn` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Definition of systemd-nspawn configurations. |
| `systemd.nspawn.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. `serial-getty@ttyS0`) from being started. Note that `enable=true` does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see `wantedBy`. |
| `systemd.nspawn.<name>.execConfig` | `attribute set of (systemd option)` | Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the `[Exec]` section of this unit. See {manpage}`systemd.nspawn(5)` for details. |
| `systemd.nspawn.<name>.filesConfig` | `attribute set of (systemd option)` | Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the `[Files]` section of this unit. See {manpage}`systemd.nspawn(5)` for details. |
| `systemd.nspawn.<name>.networkConfig` | `attribute set of (systemd option)` | Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the `[Network]` section of this unit. See {manpage}`systemd.nspawn(5)` for details. |
| `systemd.oomd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the `systemd-oomd` OOM killer. |
| `systemd.oomd.enableRootSlice` | `boolean` | Whether to enable oomd on the root slice (`-.slice`). |
| `systemd.oomd.enableSystemSlice` | `boolean` | Whether to enable oomd on the system slice (`system.slice`). |
| `systemd.oomd.enableUserSlices` | `boolean` | Whether to enable oomd on all user slices (`user@.slice`) and all user owned slices. |
| `systemd.oomd.settings.OOM` | `open submodule of attribute set of (systemd option)` | Settings option for systemd-oomd. See {manpage}`oomd.conf(5)` for available options. |
| `systemd.package` | `package` | The systemd package to use. |
| `systemd.packages` | `list of package` | Packages providing systemd units and hooks. |
| `systemd.paths` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Definition of systemd path units; see {manpage}`systemd.path(5)`. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.after` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay this unit until they have started. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.aliases` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Aliases of that unit. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.before` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay them until this unit has started. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.bindsTo` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Like â€˜requiresâ€™, but in addition, if the specified units unexpectedly disappear, this unit will be stopped as well. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.conflicts` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are started, then this unit is stopped and vice versa. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.description` | `(optionally newline-terminated) single-line string` | Description of this unit used in systemd messages and progress indicators. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.documentation` | `list of string` | A list of URIs referencing documentation for this unit or its configuration. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. `serial-getty@ttyS0`) from being started. Note that `enable=true` does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see `wantedBy`. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.name` | `string` | The name of this systemd unit, including its extension. This can be used to refer to this unit from other systemd units. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.onFailure` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "failed" state. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.onSuccess` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "inactive" state. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.overrideStrategy` | `one of "asDropinIfExists", "asDropin"` | Defines how unit configuration is provided for systemd: `asDropinIfExists` creates a unit file when no unit file is provided by the package otherwise it creates a drop-in file named `overrides.conf`. `asDropin` creates a drop-in file named `overrides.conf`. Mainly needed to define instances for systemd template units (e.g. `systemd-nspawn@mycontainer.service`). See also {manpage}`systemd.unit(5)`. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.partOf` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are stopped or restarted, then this unit is stopped or restarted as well. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.pathConfig` | `attribute set of (systemd option)` | Each attribute in this set specifies an option in the `[Path]` section of the unit. See {manpage}`systemd.path(5)` for details. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.reloadTriggers` | `list of (systemd option)` | An arbitrary list of items such as derivations. If any item in the list changes between reconfigurations, the service will be reloaded. If anything but a reload trigger changes in the unit file, the unit will be restarted instead. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.requiredBy` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Units that require (i.e. depend on and need to go down with) this unit. As discussed in the `wantedBy` option description this also creates `.requires` symlinks automatically. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.requires` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Start the specified units when this unit is started, and stop this unit when the specified units are stopped or fail. |
| `systemd.paths.<name>.requisite` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Similar to requires. However if the units listed are not started, they will not be started and the transaction will fail. |

| `systemd.paths.<name>.startLimitIntervalSec` | `signed integer` | Configure unit start rate limiting. Units which are started more than startLimitBurst times within an interval time interval are not permitted to start any more. |

| `systemd.paths.<name>.upheldBy` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Keep this unit running as long as the listed units are running. This is a continuously enforced version of wantedBy. |

The default method for starting a unit by default at boot time is to set this option to `["multi-user.target"]` for system services. Likewise for user units (`systemd.user.<name>.*`) set it to `["default.target"]` to make a unit start by default when the user `<name>` logs on. This option creates a `.wants` symlink in the given target that exists statelessly without the need for running `systemctl enable`. The `[Install]` section described in {manpage}`systemd.unit(5)` however is not supported because it is a stateful process that does not fit well into the NixOS design. |

| `systemd.repart.partitions` | `attribute set of attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)` | Specify partitions as a set of the names of the definition files as the key and the partition configuration as its value. The partition configuration can use all upstream options. See {manpage}`repart.d(5)` for all available options. |

| `systemd.services.<name>.aliases` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Aliases of that unit. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.before` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are started at the same time as this unit, delay them until this unit has started. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.bindsTo` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Like â€˜requiresâ€™, but in addition, if the specified units unexpectedly disappear, this unit will be stopped as well. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.confinement.binSh` | `null or absolute path` | The program to make available as {file}`/bin/sh` inside the chroot. If this is set to `null`, no {file}`/bin/sh` is provided at all. This is useful for some applications, which for example use the {manpage}`system(3)` library function to execute commands. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.confinement.enable` | `boolean` | If set, all the required runtime store paths for this service are bind-mounted into a `tmpfs`-based {manpage}`chroot(2)`. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.confinement.fullUnit` | `boolean` | Whether to include the full closure of the systemd unit file into the chroot, instead of just the dependencies for the executables. ::: {.warning} While it may be tempting to just enable this option to make things work quickly, please be aware that this might add paths to the closure of the chroot that you didn't anticipate. It's better to use {option}`confinement.packages` to **explicitly** add additional store paths to the chroot. ::: |
| `systemd.services.<name>.confinement.mode` | `one of "full-apivfs", "chroot-only"` | The value `full-apivfs` (the default) sets up private {file}`/dev`, {file}`/proc`, {file}`/sys`, {file}`/tmp` and {file}`/var/tmp` file systems in a separate user name space. If this is set to `chroot-only`, only the file system name space is set up along with the call to {manpage}`chroot(2)`. In all cases, unless `serviceConfig.PrivateTmp=true` is set, both {file}`/tmp` and {file}`/var/tmp` paths are added to `InaccessiblePaths=`. This is to overcome options like `DynamicUser=true` implying `PrivateTmp=true` without letting it being turned off. Beware however that giving processes the `CAP_SYS_ADMIN` and `@mount` privileges can let them undo the effects of `InaccessiblePaths=`. ::: {.note} This doesn't cover network namespaces and is solely for file system level isolation. ::: |
| `systemd.services.<name>.confinement.packages` | `list of (string or package)` | Additional packages or strings with context to add to the closure of the chroot. By default, this includes all the packages from the {option}`serviceConfig.ExecReload`, {option}`serviceConfig.ExecStartPost`, {option}`serviceConfig.ExecStartPre`, {option}`serviceConfig.ExecStop`, {option}`serviceConfig.ExecStopPost` and {option}`serviceConfig.ExecStart` options. If you want to have all the dependencies of this systemd unit, you can use {option}`confinement.fullUnit`. ::: {.note} The store paths listed in {option}`path` are **not** included in the closure as well as paths from other options except those listed above. ::: |
| `systemd.services.<name>.conflicts` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | If the specified units are started, then this unit is stopped and vice versa. |

| `systemd.services.<name>.documentation` | `list of string` | A list of URIs referencing documentation for this unit or its configuration. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. `serial-getty@ttyS0`) from being started. Note that `enable=true` does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see `wantedBy`. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.enableDefaultPath` | `boolean` | Whether to append a minimal default {env}`PATH` environment variable to the service, containing common system utilities. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.enableStrictShellChecks` | `boolean` | Enable running `shellcheck` on the generated scripts for this unit. When enabled, scripts generated by the unit will be checked with `shellcheck` and any errors or warnings will cause the build to fail. This affects all scripts that have been created through the `script`, `reload`, `preStart`, `postStart`, `preStop` and `postStop` options for systemd services. This does not affect command lines passed directly to `ExecStart`, `ExecReload`, `ExecStartPre`, `ExecStartPost`, `ExecStop` or `ExecStopPost`. |

| `systemd.services.<name>.notSocketActivated` | `boolean` | If set, a changed unit is never assumed to be socket-activated on configuration switch, even if it might have associated socket units. Instead, the unit will be restarted (or stopped/started) as if it had no associated sockets. |
| `systemd.services.<name>.onFailure` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | A list of one or more units that are activated when this unit enters the "failed" state. |

Mainly needed to define instances for systemd template units (e.g. `systemd-nspawn@mycontainer.service`). See also {manpage}`systemd.unit(5)`. |

| `systemd.units.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. `serial-getty@ttyS0`) from being started. Note that `enable=true` does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see `wantedBy`. |

The default method for starting a unit by default at boot time is to set this option to `["multi-user.target"]` for system services. Likewise for user units (`systemd.user.<name>.*`) set it to `["default.target"]` to make a unit start by default when the user `<name>` logs on. This option creates a `.wants` symlink in the given target that exists statelessly without the need for running `systemctl enable`. | `systemd.user.tmpfiles.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable systemd user units systemd-tmpfiles-setup.service and systemd-tmpfiles-clean.timer. |
| `systemd.user.tmpfiles.rules` | `list of string` | Global user rules for creation, deletion and cleaning of volatile and temporary files automatically. See {manpage}`tmpfiles.d(5)` for the exact format. |
| `systemd.user.tmpfiles.users` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Per-user rules for creation, deletion and cleaning of volatile and temporary files automatically. |
| `systemd.user.tmpfiles.users.<name>.rules` | `list of string` | Per-user rules for creation, deletion and cleaning of volatile and temporary files automatically. See {manpage}`tmpfiles.d(5)` for the exact format. |
| `systemd.user.units` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Definition of systemd per-user units. |
| `systemd.user.units.<name>.aliases` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Aliases of that unit. |
| `systemd.user.units.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | If set to false, this unit will be a symlink to /dev/null. This is primarily useful to prevent specific template instances (e.g. `serial-getty@ttyS0`) from being started. Note that `enable=true` does not make a unit start by default at boot; if you want that, see `wantedBy`. |
| `systemd.user.units.<name>.name` | `string` | The name of this systemd unit, including its extension. This can be used to refer to this unit from other systemd units. |
| `systemd.user.units.<name>.overrideStrategy` | `one of "asDropinIfExists", "asDropin"` | Defines how unit configuration is provided for systemd: `asDropinIfExists` creates a unit file when no unit file is provided by the package otherwise it creates a drop-in file named `overrides.conf`. `asDropin` creates a drop-in file named `overrides.conf`. Mainly needed to define instances for systemd template units (e.g. `systemd-nspawn@mycontainer.service`). See also {manpage}`systemd.unit(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.units.<name>.requiredBy` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Units that require (i.e. depend on and need to go down with) this unit. As discussed in the `wantedBy` option description this also creates `.requires` symlinks automatically. |
| `systemd.user.units.<name>.text` | `null or string` | Text of this systemd unit. |
| `systemd.user.units.<name>.upheldBy` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Keep this unit running as long as the listed units are running. This is a continuously enforced version of wantedBy. |
| `systemd.user.units.<name>.wantedBy` | `list of string matching the pattern [a-zA-Z0-9@%:_.\\-]+[.](service\|socket\|device\|mount\|automount\|swap\|target\|path\|timer\|scope\|slice)` | Units that want (i.e. depend on) this unit. The default method for starting a unit by default at boot time is to set this option to `["multi-user.target"]` for system services. Likewise for user units (`systemd.user.<name>.*`) set it to `["default.target"]` to make a unit start by default when the user `<name>` logs on. This option creates a `.wants` symlink in the given target that exists statelessly without the need for running `systemctl enable`. The `[Install]` section described in {manpage}`systemd.unit(5)` however is not supported because it is a stateful process that does not fit well into the NixOS design. |
